`cat README.md 2>/dev/null | head -60; echo "==="; find . -name "*.py" -not -path "*/node_modules/*" -not -path "*/.git/*" -not -path "*/venv/*" -not -path "*/.venv/*" | head -50`

```text
# translate

A fast CLI/TUI translation tool for the terminal — one-shot from the shell or an
interactive live-translating TUI. Backed by an auto fallback chain over your
local LLM providers (copilot-proxy → Ollama), a free web API (Google), and a
dictionary, with typo-tolerant language resolution and translation history.

## Install

```sh
brew install daviddwlee84/tap/translate               # macOS/Linux via Homebrew (builds from source)
go install github.com/daviddwlee84/translate@latest   # or with a Go toolchain, into $GOBIN (~/.local/bin)
```

## Build & run

```sh
go build -o translate .      # Go 1.25+ (developed on 1.26)
./translate "hola mundo" --to en          # one-shot
echo "bonjour" | ./translate --to en      # pipe
./translate                               # interactive TUI
```

First run writes a default config to `~/.config/translate/config.toml`; run
`./translate init` for a guided setup that probes which providers are up.

## Usage

| Command | What it does |
|---|---|
| `translate <text>` / `… \| translate` | One-shot translation (streams to a TTY; plain text when piped) |
| `translate` (no args, TTY) | Interactive TUI |
| `translate --to <lang> --from <lang>` | Language override; both are fuzzy (`chinees` → `zh`) |
| `translate --json` | Emit the full structured result |
| `… \| translate --bilingual` (`-2`) | Bilingual pipe view: keep the original (with color) + translation beneath (stdin only) |
| `translate define <word>` | Dictionary lookup (bilingual: zh↔en local, or English API) |
| `translate history` / `history search <q>` | Recent history / fuzzy search (`--tsv`, `--json`) |
| `translate init` | Interactive config wizard (probes providers) |
| `translate config path\|show` · `lang resolve <q>` | Introspection helpers |

Flags: `--engine smartauto|auto|<provider>|google`, `--provider`, `--model`, `--tier default|fast|max`, `--preset concise|contextual|dictionary`, `--instructions`, `--pair`/`--pair-with`, `--bilingual`/`-2`, `--no-history`, `--debug`.
Env overrides: `TRANSLATE_TARGET`, `TRANSLATE_SOURCE`, `TRANSLATE_ENGINE`, `TRANSLATE_PROVIDER`, `TRANSLATE_MODEL`, `TRANSLATE_CONFIG`, `TRANSLATE_DEBUG`.
Precedence: **flag > env > `[cli]`/`[tui]` > `[general]` > default**.

### Per-front-end defaults (`[cli]` / `[tui]`)

The CLI (arguments or piped stdin) and the interactive TUI share `[general]`, but
either can override any subset of it via an optional `[cli]` or `[tui]` table — e.g.
a snappy concise CLI and a richer, higher-tier TUI:

```toml
[general]
preset = "contextual"
tier   = "fast"

[cli]                 # one-shot / piped translation
preset = "concise"

[tui]                 # interactive front-end
tier          = "max"
===
./.agents/skills/agent-history-hygiene/tests/conftest.py
./.agents/skills/agent-history-hygiene/tests/test_redact_secrets.py
./.agents/skills/agent-history-hygiene/tests/test_gitleaks_corpus.py
./.agents/skills/agent-history-hygiene/assets/redact_secrets.py
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-22 04:50:25Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Clean pty capture of single vs multi word

```bash
cd /tmp; cat > /tmp/ptyrun.py <<'PY'
import pty, os, sys, select

def run(argv):
    out = []
    pid, fd = pty.fork()
    if pid == 0:  # child
        os.execvp(argv[0], argv)
    else:
        while True:
            try:
                r, _, _ = select.select([fd], [], [], 5)
            except OSError:
                break
            if fd in r:
                try:
                    data = os.read(fd, 1024)
                except OSError:
                    break
                if not data:
                    break
                out.append(data)
            else:
                break
        os.waitpid(pid, 0)
    return b"".join(out)

data = run(sys.argv[1:])
sys.stdout.write("--- repr ---\n")
sys.stdout.write(repr(data))
sys.stdout.write("\n--- rendered ---\n")
sys.stdout.buffer.write(data)
sys.stdout.write("\n--- len=%d ---\n" % len(data))
PY
echo "===== SINGLE WORD (dictionary path) ====="; python3 /tmp/ptyrun.py /tmp/translate directive
echo; echo "===== MULTI WORD (llm path) ====="; python3 /tmp/ptyrun.py /tmp/translate "good morning everyone"
```

54	// NewRootCmd builds the root command and its subcommands.
55	func NewRootCmd() *cobra.Command {
56		root := &cobra.Command{
57			Use:   "translate [text...]",
58			Short: "Fast terminal translation (CLI + TUI)",
59			Long: "translate is a fast CLI/TUI translator.\n\n" +
60				"  translate \"hola mundo\" --to en    one-shot\n" +
61				"  echo hola | translate --to en       pipe\n" +
62				"  translate                           interactive TUI",
63			Version:       buildVersion(),
64			SilenceUsage:  true,
65			SilenceErrors: true,
66			// ArbitraryArgs lets bare text fall through to translation while exact
67			// subcommands (config, lang, …) still route. Without it, cobra rejects
68			// `translate "hola"` as an unknown command once subcommands exist.
69			Args: cobra.ArbitraryArgs,
70			RunE: runRoot,
71		}
72		f := root.PersistentFlags()
73		f.StringVarP(&flagTo, "to", "t", "", "target language (fuzzy; e.g. en, chinese)")
74		f.StringVarP(&flagFrom, "from", "f", "", "source language (\"auto\" to detect)")
75		f.StringVar(&flagModel, "model", "", "LLM model id override")
76		f.StringVar(&flagProvider, "provider", "", "provider name (e.g. copilot, ollama)")
77		f.StringVar(&flagEngine, "engine", "", "engine: auto|<provider>|google|dict")
78		f.StringVar(&flagTier, "tier", "", "model tier: default|fast|max")
79		f.StringVar(&flagPreset, "preset", "", "LLM prompt style: concise|contextual|dictionary")
80		f.StringVar(&flagInstructions, "instructions", "", "extra system-prompt guidance (domain focus, etc.)")
81		f.BoolVar(&flagPair, "pair", false, "bidirectional mode: home-language input → --pair-with, else → --to")
82		f.StringVar(&flagPairWith, "pair-with", "", "the other language for --pair (e.g. en)")
83		f.BoolVar(&flagLearn, "learn", false, "learning mode: teach (native→foreign) or grammar-correct (foreign→native)")
84		f.BoolVarP(&flagBilingual, "bilingual", "2", false, "bilingual pipe mode: keep original (with color) + translation beneath (stdin only)")
85		f.StringVar(&flagBilingualMode, "bilingual-mode", "doc", "bilingual strategy: doc (context-aware, one LLM call) | blocks (per-block)")
86		f.BoolVar(&flagJSON, "json", false, "emit the full result as JSON")
87		f.BoolVar(&flagNoHistory, "no-history", false, "do not record this translation in history")
88		f.BoolVar(&flagDebug, "debug", false, "log intermediate decisions (routing, engine choice, dict hit/miss)")
89		f.BoolVarP(&flagSpeak, "speak", "s", false, "speak the foreign side of the result aloud (free TTS)")
90		f.StringVar(&flagSpeakLang, "speak-lang", "", "force the spoken language (e.g. en, zh-TW)")
91	
92		root.SuggestionsMinimumDistance = 2
93		root.AddCommand(newConfigCmd(), newLangCmd(), newDefineCmd(), newHistoryCmd(), newInitCmd(), newDictCmd(), newSpeakCmd())
94		return root
95	}
96	
97	// Execute runs the root command with the given (cancellable) context.
98	func Execute(ctx context.Context) error {
99		// Stamp the writing app version into any config Save this run makes.
100		config.Generator = shortVersion()
101		return NewRootCmd().ExecuteContext(ctx)
102	}
103	
104	// overrides collects the explicitly-set flag values.
105	func overrides() config.Overrides {
106		return config.Overrides{
107			Source:       flagFrom,
108			Target:       flagTo,
109			Engine:       flagEngine,
110			Provider:     flagProvider,
111			Model:        flagModel,
112			Tier:         flagTier,
113			Preset:       flagPreset,
114			Instructions: flagInstructions,
115			Pair:         flagPair,
116			PairWith:     flagPairWith,
117			Learn:        flagLearn,
118			Debug:        flagDebug,
119		}
120	}
121	
122	// invocationMode reports whether this run is the one-shot CLI (arguments or piped
123	// stdin) or the interactive TUI (a TTY with no arguments). It mirrors the dispatch
124	// switch in runRoot and is computed before Resolve so the [cli]/[tui] overlay applies.
125	func invocationMode(args []string) config.Mode {
126		if len(args) > 0 {
127			return config.ModeCLI
128		}
129		if !term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdin.Fd())) {
130			return config.ModeCLI
131		}
132		return config.ModeTUI
133	}
134	
135	func runRoot(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
136		ctx := cmd.Context()
137	
138		cfg, created, err := config.Load()
139		if err != nil {
140			return err
141		}
142		if created {
143			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: wrote default config to %s (run `translate init` to customize)\n", config.Path())
144		} else if cfg.Outdated() {
145			// Schema bumps so far are additive: Load already filled any new fields with
146			// their Default() values in memory, so re-saving materializes them on disk
147			// (every existing value preserved) and re-stamps the schema — no wizard
148			// needed. A genuinely breaking change would instead need an explicit
149			// migration here before the re-save.
150			from := cfg.Schema
151			if err := config.Save(cfg); err != nil {
152				fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: config is outdated but auto-upgrade failed (%v) — run `translate init`\n", err)
153			} else {
154				fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: upgraded config schema v%d→v%d (new settings use defaults; `translate init` to review)\n", from, config.SchemaVersion)
155			}
156		}
157	
158		res := cfg.Resolve(overrides(), invocationMode(args))
159		if res.Debug {
160			// The one-shot CLI logs to stderr; the TUI logs to a file, since its
161			// alt-screen would be corrupted by stderr writes.
162			if invocationMode(args) == config.ModeCLI {
163				debug.Enable(os.Stderr)
164			} else if w := openDebugLog(); w != nil {
165				debug.Enable(w)
166			}
167		}
168		applyLastPair(cfg, &res) // remember_last_pair: seed source/target from state
169		src, tgt := resolvePair(res.Source, res.Target)
170		pairWith := ""
171		if res.Pair {
172			pwm, _ := lang.Resolve(res.PairWith)
173			pairWith = pwm.Code
174		}
175		// Learn mode is bidirectional; ensure a distinct "away" (foreign) language so
176		// direction routing has two sides to choose between.
177		if res.Learn && (pairWith == "" || strings.EqualFold(pairWith, tgt)) {
178			pairWith = defaultAway(tgt)
179		}
180		debug.Logf("resolved: engine=%s tier=%s preset=%s source=%s target=%s pair=%v pair_with=%s learn=%v",
181			res.Engine, res.Tier, res.Preset, src, tgt, res.Pair, pairWith, res.Learn)
182		if res.Pair && !res.Learn && (pairWith == "" || strings.EqualFold(pairWith, tgt)) {
183			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: warning: pair mode is on but pair-with (%q) equals the target (%q) — pair mode is a no-op; set a different pair_with (run `translate init`)\n", pairWith, tgt)
184		}
185	
186		if res.Provider == nil && res.Engine != "auto" {
187			return fmt.Errorf("no provider configured; check %s", config.Path())
188		}
189		eng, err := buildEngine(res)
190		if err != nil {
191			return err
192		}
193	
194		// The one-shot CLI path routes a learn request through a bare LLM engine
195		// (bypassing smart-auto/dictionary); the TUI keeps its normal primary engine and
196		// toggles learn at runtime against Params.LearnEngine.
197		learnCLI := res.Learn && invocationMode(args) == config.ModeCLI
198		if learnCLI && res.Provider == nil {
199			return fmt.Errorf("learn mode requires an LLM provider; check %s", config.Path())
200		}
201		oneShotEng := eng
202		if learnCLI {
203			oneShotEng = learnEngineFromConfig(res)
204		}
205	
206		st := openStore(cfg)
207		if st != nil {
208			defer st.Close()
209		}
210	
211		switch {
212		case len(args) > 0:
213			text := strings.Join(args, " ")
214			effTgt := effectiveTarget(res.Pair, tgt, pairWith, text)
215			r, err := oneShot(ctx, oneShotEng, text, src, effTgt, res.Stream, res.Preset, res.Instructions, res.Pair, tgt, pairWith, res.Learn)
216			if err != nil {
217				return err
218			}
219			recordAndRemember(ctx, st, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, tgt)
220			if shouldSpeak(cfg) {
221				speakResult(ctx, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, speakForeign(cfg, pairWith))
222			}
223			return nil
224	
225		case !term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdin.Fd())):
226			b, err := io.ReadAll(os.Stdin)
227			if err != nil {
228				return err
229			}
230			// Strip ANSI/SGR escapes so colored piped input (e.g. `tldr rg | translate`)
231			// never pollutes the prompt. The raw bytes are retained only for --bilingual,
232			// which needs the original styling for display.
233			text := strings.TrimSpace(bitext.Strip(string(b)))
234			if text == "" {
235				return fmt.Errorf("no input on stdin")
236			}
237			// Bilingual is a multi-block reading view; --json/--learn keep their own
238			// structured output and take precedence.
239			if flagBilingual && !flagJSON && !res.Learn {
240				// doc mode needs an LLM (structured JSON, empty Text confuses
241				// smartauto/chain routing) — build a bare LLM like learn mode; nil when
242				// no provider, in which case runBilingual falls back to per-block.
243				var docEng engine.Engine
244				if res.Provider != nil {
245					docEng = learnEngineFromConfig(res)
246				}
247				return runBilingual(ctx, oneShotEng, docEng, string(b), src, tgt, res.Instructions, flagBilingualMode)
248			}
249			effTgt := effectiveTarget(res.Pair, tgt, pairWith, text)
250			r, err := oneShot(ctx, oneShotEng, text, src, effTgt, res.Stream, res.Preset, res.Instructions, res.Pair, tgt, pairWith, res.Learn)
251			if err != nil {
252				return err
253			}
254			recordAndRemember(ctx, st, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, tgt)
255			if shouldSpeak(cfg) {
256				speakResult(ctx, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, speakForeign(cfg, pairWith))
257			}
258			return nil
259	
260		default:
261			return runTUI(ctx, eng, res, st, src, tgt, pairWith)
262		}
263	}
264	
265	// effectiveTarget applies pair mode: home-language input → away, else → home.
266	func effectiveTarget(pair bool, home, away, text string) string {
267		if pair && away != "" {
268			t := lang.PairTarget(home, away, text)
269			debug.Logf("pair route: home=%s away=%s text=%q → target=%s", home, away, clip(text, 30), t)
270			return t
271		}
272		return home
273	}
274	
275	// clip shortens a string for a single-line debug label.
276	func clip(s string, n int) string {
277		s = strings.TrimSpace(s)
278		r := []rune(s)
279		if len(r) > n {
280			return string(r[:n]) + "…"
281		}
282		return s
283	}
284	
285	// openDebugLog opens (append) the TUI debug log file, creating the state dir.
286	// Returns nil on failure (debug simply stays off).
287	func openDebugLog() io.Writer {
288		if err := xdgpath.EnsureDirs(); err != nil {
289			return nil
290		}
291		f, err := os.OpenFile(filepath.Join(xdgpath.StateDir(), "debug.log"),
292			os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY|os.O_APPEND, 0o600)
293		if err != nil {
294			return nil
295		}
296		return f
297	}
298	
299	// applyLastPair overrides the resolved source/target with the persisted last
300	// pair when remember_last_pair is on and the user did not pass flags/env.
301	func applyLastPair(cfg *config.Config, res *config.Resolved) {
302		if !cfg.General.RememberLastPair {
303			return
304		}
305		// Pair (and learn) mode anchors BOTH languages from config: Target is the home
306		// language, PairWith is the away language, and routing is decided per input. A
307		// remembered target must not override the home here — if the last session
308		// happened to translate into the away language, restoring it as the home makes
309		// home == away and pair mode collapses to a no-op (the "en⇄en" bug). Source is
310		// always auto-detected in pair mode too, so skip the whole override.
311		if res.Pair {
312			debug.Logf("remember_last_pair: skipped (pair mode anchors languages from config)")
313			return
314		}
315		stt, err := state.Load()
316		if err != nil || stt == nil {
317			return
318		}
319		if flagFrom == "" && os.Getenv("TRANSLATE_SOURCE") == "" && stt.Source != "" {
320			res.Source = stt.Source
321			debug.Logf("remember_last_pair: source ← %s (from state)", stt.Source)
322		}
323		if flagTo == "" && os.Getenv("TRANSLATE_TARGET") == "" && stt.Target != "" {
324			res.Target = stt.Target
325			debug.Logf("remember_last_pair: target ← %s (from state)", stt.Target)
326		}
327	}
328	
329	// openStore opens the history store, or returns nil when history is disabled or
330	// suppressed with --no-history.
331	func openStore(cfg *config.Config) store.Store {
332		if !cfg.History.Enabled || flagNoHistory {
333			return nil
334		}
335		path := cfg.History.Path
336		if path == "" {
337			path = xdgpath.HistoryFile()
338		}
339		st, err := store.OpenJSONL(path)
340		if err != nil {
341			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: history disabled (%v)\n", err)
342			return nil
343		}
344		return st
345	}
346	
347	// recordAndRemember writes the translation to history and persists the last pair.
348	//
349	// target is the effective target actually used (for history); rememberTarget is
350	// the stable home target (== target unless pair mode redirected this one input),
351	// so persisting it keeps pair mode from drifting its home to the away language.
352	func recordAndRemember(ctx context.Context, st store.Store, cfg *config.Config, res *engine.TranslateResult, input, source, target, rememberTarget string) {
353		if st != nil {
354			_, _ = st.Add(ctx, toRecord(res, input, source, target))
355		}
356		if cfg.General.RememberLastPair {
357			saveLastPair(source, rememberTarget, res.Engine)
358		}
359	}
360	
361	// toRecord builds a history Record from a translation result.
362	func toRecord(res *engine.TranslateResult, input, source, target string) store.Record {
363		src := source
364		if src == "auto" && res.DetectedSource != "" {
365			src = res.DetectedSource
366		}
367		return store.Record{
368			SourceLang:   src,
369			TargetLang:   target,
370			Engine:       res.Engine,
371			Model:        res.Model,
372			Input:        input,
373			Output:       res.Translation,
374			Alternatives: res.Alternatives,
375			Notes:        res.Notes,
376		}
377	}
378	
379	// saveLastPair persists the source/target pair and source mode.
380	func saveLastPair(source, target, engineName string) {
381		mode := "fixed"
382		if source == "auto" {
383			mode = "auto"
384		}
385		_ = state.Save(&state.State{
386			Source:     source,
387			Target:     target,
388			SourceMode: mode,
389			Engine:     engineName,
390		})
391	}
392	
393	// runTUI launches the interactive Bubble Tea front-end and persists the last
394	// pair on exit.
395	func runTUI(ctx context.Context, eng engine.Engine, res config.Resolved, st store.Store, source, target, pairWith string) error {
396		// A model source for the ^p model picker: the resolved LLM provider (if any).
397		var modelSrc engine.ModelLister
398		modelProvider := ""
399		if res.Provider != nil {
400			modelSrc = llmFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model)
401			modelProvider = res.Provider.Name
402		}
403		// Learn mode (^n) runs against a bare LLM engine; nil when no provider is
404		// configured, in which case the toggle refuses.
405		var learnEng engine.Engine
406		if res.Provider != nil {
407			learnEng = learnEngineFromConfig(res)
408		}
409		p := tui.Params{
410			Engines:       buildEngineSet(res, eng),
411			ModelSource:   modelSrc,
412			ModelProvider: modelProvider,
413			Store:         st,
414			Source:        source,
415			Target:        target,
416			Pair:          res.Pair,
417			PairWith:      pairWith,
418			Learn:         res.Learn,
419			LearnEngine:   learnEng,
420			Model:         res.Model,
421			Preset:        res.Preset,
422			Instructions:  res.Instructions,
423			Live:          res.LiveTranslate,
424			DebounceMs:    res.DebounceMs,
425			Speaker:       tuiSpeaker(res.Cfg),
426			Foreign:       speakForeign(res.Cfg, pairWith),
427		}
428		final, err := tea.NewProgram(tui.New(ctx, p), tea.WithContext(ctx)).Run()
429		if err != nil {
430			return err
431		}
432		if res.Cfg.General.RememberLastPair {
433			if fm, ok := final.(tui.Model); ok {
434				s, t := fm.Pair()
435				saveLastPair(s, t, "")
436			}
437		}
438		return nil
439	}
440	
441	// resolvePair fuzzy-resolves the source and target languages, printing an
442	// "(interpreted X as Y)" note on stderr when a fuzzy correction was applied.
443	func resolvePair(rawSource, rawTarget string) (source, target string) {
444		sm, _ := lang.Resolve(rawSource)
445		tm, _ := lang.Resolve(rawTarget)
446		if !sm.Exact && sm.Score > 0 && !strings.EqualFold(rawSource, sm.Code) {
447			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: interpreted source %q as %s (%s)\n", rawSource, sm.Code, sm.Name)
448		}
449		if !tm.Exact && tm.Score > 0 && !strings.EqualFold(rawTarget, tm.Code) {
450			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: interpreted target %q as %s (%s)\n", rawTarget, tm.Code, tm.Name)
451		}
452		return sm.Code, tm.Code
453	}
454	
455	// oneShot translates text once and prints the result, returning it for history.
456	//
457	// Tokens stream live to stdout only when stdout is a TTY (so `translate x | pbcopy`
458	// stays clean) and --json was not requested. Piped output is the plain translation
459	// with no ANSI; --json emits the full structured result.
460	func oneShot(ctx context.Context, eng engine.Engine, text, source, target string, streamPref bool, preset, instructions string, pair bool, pairHome, pairAway string, learn bool) (*engine.TranslateResult, error) {
461		stdoutTTY := term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdout.Fd()))
462		stream := streamPref && stdoutTTY && !flagJSON && !learn // learn output is structured (parsed at done)
463	
464		req := engine.Request{
465			Text:     text,
466			Source:   source,
467			Target:   target,
468			Mode:     engine.ModeTranslate,
469			Stream:   stream,
470			Preset:   preset,
471			Extra:    instructions,
472			Pair:     pair,
473			PairHome: pairHome,
474			PairAway: pairAway,
475			Learn:    learn,
476		}
477		ch, err := eng.Translate(ctx, req)
478		if err != nil {
479			return nil, err
480		}
481	
482		var onTok func(string)
483		if stream {
484			onTok = func(t string) { fmt.Print(t) }
485		}
486		res, err := engine.Drain(ch, onTok)
487		if err != nil {
488			if stream {
489				fmt.Println() // terminate any partial line before the error surfaces
490			}
491			return nil, err
492		}
493	
494		// Never downgrade silently: if the auto-chain fell back, say which engine
495		// failed and why, and how to switch.
496		for _, w := range res.Warnings {
497			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: warning: %s\n", w)
498		}
499		if len(res.Warnings) > 0 && res.Learn == nil {
500			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: used %q (switch with --engine, or check the model/provider)\n", res.Engine)
501		}
502	
503		switch {
504		case flagJSON:
505			enc := json.NewEncoder(os.Stdout)
506			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
507			if err := enc.Encode(res); err != nil {
508				return nil, err
509			}
510		case res.Learn != nil:
511			fmt.Print(renderLearnCLI(res))
512		case stream:
513			fmt.Println() // newline after the streamed tokens
514		default:
515			fmt.Println(res.Translation)
516		}
517		return res, nil
518	}
519	
520	// runBilingual renders the "immersive" bilingual view for piped input: every
521	// original block is printed verbatim (ANSI/color intact) and a translation is shown
522	// beneath each prose block; indented command/code blocks are echoed untranslated.
523	// mode "doc" (default) translates the whole document in one context-aware call via
524	// docEng (a bare LLM); "blocks" (or a doc-mode fallback) translates each prose block
525	// in isolation via blockEng. Pair and learn routing are out of scope for this view.
526	func runBilingual(ctx context.Context, blockEng, docEng engine.Engine, raw, source, target, instructions, mode string) error {
527		blocks := bitext.Split(raw)
528		if len(blocks) == 0 {
529			return fmt.Errorf("no input on stdin")
530		}
531	
532		var translations map[int]string
533		switch mode {
534		case "blocks":
535			translations = bilingualBlocks(ctx, blockEng, blocks, source, target, instructions)
536		default: // "doc"
537			if docEng != nil {
538				if t, ok := bilingualDoc(ctx, docEng, blocks, source, target, instructions); ok {
539					translations = t
540				}
541			}
542			if translations == nil { // no LLM provider, or the doc call failed/was unparseable
543				fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "translate: bilingual: doc mode unavailable, using per-block")
544				translations = bilingualBlocks(ctx, blockEng, blocks, source, target, instructions)
545			}
546		}
547	
548		dim := dimFunc(term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdout.Fd())))
549		fmt.Print(bitext.Render(blocks, translations, dim))
550		return nil
551	}
552	
553	// bilingualBlocks translates each prose block in isolation (concurrent, bounded).
554	// The original strategy: works with any engine, but has no cross-block context.
555	func bilingualBlocks(ctx context.Context, eng engine.Engine, blocks []bitext.Block, source, target, instructions string) map[int]string {
556		translations := make(map[int]string)
557		errs := make(map[int]error)
558		var mu sync.Mutex
559		var wg sync.WaitGroup
560		sem := make(chan struct{}, 5) // bound concurrent LLM calls
561	
562		for i, blk := range blocks {
563			if blk.Kind != bitext.Prose {
564				continue
565			}
566			wg.Add(1)
567			go func(i int, plain string) {
568				defer wg.Done()
569				sem <- struct{}{}
570				defer func() { <-sem }()
571	
572				// Force concise (Stream:false, Preset:"") so each block maps to exactly
573				// one translation — contextual/dictionary presets would reshape output.
574				r, err := translateOnce(ctx, eng, engine.Request{
575					Text:   plain,
576					Source: source,
577					Target: target,
578					Mode:   engine.ModeTranslate,
579					Extra:  instructions,
580				})
581				mu.Lock()
582				defer mu.Unlock()
583				if err != nil {
584					errs[i] = err
585					return
586				}
587				tr := strings.TrimSpace(r.Translation)
588				// Drop echoes (proper nouns / already-target text): nothing to add beneath.
589				if tr != "" && !strings.EqualFold(tr, strings.TrimSpace(plain)) {
590					translations[i] = tr
591				}
592			}(i, blk.Plain)
593		}
594		wg.Wait()
595	
596		// Never fail silently: report untranslated blocks (originals still printed).
597		if len(errs) > 0 {
598			var sample error
599			for _, e := range errs {
600				sample = e
601				break
602			}
603			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: warning: %d block(s) not translated (%v)\n", len(errs), sample)
604		}
605		return translations
606	}
607	
608	// bilingualDoc translates the whole document in ONE context-aware structured call:
609	// the model sees every block (prose + code as context) and returns a JSON map of
610	// prose-segment number → translation. Returns ok=false when structured output isn't
611	// available (no LLM, error, or unparseable/empty reply) so the caller falls back.
612	func bilingualDoc(ctx context.Context, eng engine.Engine, blocks []bitext.Block, source, target, instructions string) (map[int]string, bool) {
613		var segs []engine.Segment
614		proseBlock := make(map[int]int) // prose-segment number (1-based) → block index
615		n := 0
616		for i, blk := range blocks {
617			switch blk.Kind {
618			case bitext.Prose:
619				n++
620				proseBlock[n] = i
621				segs = append(segs, engine.Segment{Text: blk.Plain})
622			case bitext.Code:
623				segs = append(segs, engine.Segment{Text: blk.Plain, Code: true})
624			}
625		}
626		if n == 0 {
627			return map[int]string{}, true // nothing to translate
628		}
629	
630		r, err := translateOnce(ctx, eng, engine.Request{
631			Bilingual: true,
632			Segments:  segs,
633			Source:    source,
634			Target:    target,
635			Mode:      engine.ModeTranslate,
636			Extra:     instructions,
637		})
638		if err != nil {
639			debug.Logf("bilingual doc: LLM call failed: %v", err)
640			return nil, false
641		}
642		if r == nil || len(r.Bilingual) == 0 {
643			warn := ""
644			if r != nil && len(r.Warnings) > 0 {
645				warn = r.Warnings[0]
646			}
647			debug.Logf("bilingual doc: empty/unparseable structured result (warnings: %q)", warn)
648			return nil, false
649		}
650	
651		translations := make(map[int]string)
652		for num, tr := range r.Bilingual {
653			bi, ok := proseBlock[num]
654			if !ok {
655				continue
656			}
657			t := strings.TrimSpace(tr)
658			if t != "" && !strings.EqualFold(t, strings.TrimSpace(blocks[bi].Plain)) {
659				translations[bi] = t
660			}
661		}
662		return translations, true
663	}
664	
665	// translateOnce runs a single non-streaming translation and returns the result,
666	// draining the engine channel with no token callback. It is the printing-free core
667	// shared by bilingual mode.
668	func translateOnce(ctx context.Context, eng engine.Engine, req engine.Request) (*engine.TranslateResult, error) {
669		ch, err := eng.Translate(ctx, req)
670		if err != nil {
671			return nil, err
672		}
673		return engine.Drain(ch, nil)
674	}
675	
676	// dimFunc returns the styler for bilingual translation lines. It dims (grey) only
677	// when stdout is a TTY and NO_COLOR is unset, so piping the output onward stays
678	// clean; otherwise it is the identity function. The original block's own ANSI is
679	// never touched — only our added translation lines are styled.
680	func dimFunc(stdoutTTY bool) func(string) string {
681		if !stdoutTTY || os.Getenv("NO_COLOR") != "" {
682			return func(s string) string { return s }
683		}
684		style := lg.NewStyle().Foreground(lg.Color("#6C6C6C"))
685		return func(s string) string { return style.Render(s) }
686	}
687	
688	// defaultAway picks the "away" (foreign) language for learn/pair mode when none is
689	// configured: English speakers default to learning zh-TW, everyone else to en. It
690	// mirrors the TUI ^g heuristic so the CLI and TUI agree.
691	func defaultAway(home string) string {
692		if strings.HasPrefix(strings.ToLower(home), "en") {
693			return "zh-TW"
694		}
695		return "en"
696	}
697	
698	// renderLearnCLI formats a learn-mode result as plain text (no ANSI, pipe-safe).
699	func renderLearnCLI(res *engine.TranslateResult) string {
700		l := res.Learn
701		if l == nil {
702			return res.Translation + "\n"
703		}
704		var b strings.Builder
705		if l.Direction == "correct" {
706			corrected := strings.TrimSpace(l.Corrected)
707			if corrected == "" {
708				corrected = res.Translation
709			}
710			b.WriteString("✔ " + corrected + "\n")
711			if s := strings.TrimSpace(l.Translation); s != "" {
712				b.WriteString("  ↳ " + s + "\n")
713			}
714			for _, is := range l.Issues {
715				frag := strings.TrimSpace(strings.TrimSuffix(strings.TrimPrefix(is.Span+" → "+is.Fix, " → "), " → "))
716				expl := strings.TrimSpace(is.Explanation)
717				line := expl
718				switch {
719				case frag != "" && expl != "":
720					line = frag + ": " + expl
721				case frag != "":
722					line = frag
723				}
724				if line != "" {
725					b.WriteString("  ✎ " + line + "\n")
726				}
727			}
728		} else {
729			tr := strings.TrimSpace(l.Translation)
730			if tr == "" {
731				tr = res.Translation
732			}
733			b.WriteString(tr + "\n")
734			for _, v := range l.Vocab {
735				head := "  • " + v.Term
736				if v.Pos != "" {
737					head += " (" + v.Pos + ")"
738				}
739				if v.Phonetic != "" {
740					head += " " + v.Phonetic
741				}
742				if v.Meaning != "" {
743					head += " — " + v.Meaning
744				}
745				b.WriteString(head + "\n")
746			}
747			for _, ex := range l.Examples {
748				b.WriteString("  ✎ " + ex.Foreign + "\n")
749				if s := strings.TrimSpace(ex.Native); s != "" {
750					b.WriteString("    ↳ " + s + "\n")
751				}
752			}
753		}
754		if s := strings.TrimSpace(l.Notes); s != "" {
755			b.WriteString("  ⓘ " + s + "\n")
756		}
757		return b.String()
758	}
759
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-22 04:50:48Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I'm tracing through the `oneShot` function to understand how it handles a single-word dictionary result in TTY mode. When streaming preference is enabled and we're in a TTY environment without JSON output or learning mode, the stream flag gets set to true, which then gets passed to the translation engine that routes to the dictionary handler.

The issue is that the dictionary engine doesn't emit streaming tokens like an LLM would — it returns the complete result in the final message without calling the token callback. So when the code reaches the switch statement after draining, it hits the `case stream:` branch which just prints a newline, assuming the translation was already streamed out via token callbacks. Since no tokens were actually emitted, the translation never gets printed, leaving only the newline character in the output.

I need to examine how the dictionary and smart-dict engines handle the Stream flag and whether they emit tokens when streaming is enabled.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-22 04:50:49Z)**_

`oneShot` 找到問題核心了。追蹤 TTY + 字典結果的流程：

- `stream = streamPref && stdoutTTY && !flagJSON && !learn` → 在 TTY 下為 `true`
- 字典引擎（smart-dict）不是 LLM，**不會逐字 emit token**，所以 `onTok` 從沒被呼叫 → drain 期間什麼都沒印
- 最後 switch 命中 `case stream: fmt.Println()` → **只印一個換行**，譯文從未輸出

這就解釋了 `\r\n`。我確認一下字典引擎在 `Stream:true` 時到底 emit 不 emit token，以及 `Drain` 的行為。

5	// the one-shot CLI and the Bubble Tea TUI drive the identical Engine values and

11	// Mode selects what kind of lookup a Request performs.

13

18		ModeDict

20	
21	// Request is the single input to every engine. A Source of "" or "auto" asks

33		ModelProvider string

35		Extra         string // extra user instructions appended to the system prompt
36	
37		// Pair marks a bidirectional "pair" request: the text is written in one of
38		// two languages (PairHome/PairAway) and must be translated into the OTHER.
39		// The LLM translate prompt uses this to detect-and-route and, critically, to
40		// never echo the input unchanged. Target still carries the caller's routed
41		// best guess (for history/display and non-LLM engines).

44		PairAway string // the other pair language (e.g. "en")

48		// is translated-and-taught (glosses + examples), foreign-language input is
49		// grammar-corrected-and-explained. The LLM engine answers with a strict JSON
50		// object (parsed into TranslateResult.Learn) and, because the output is

163		Meaning  string `json:"meaning"`            // meaning in the NATIVE language
164	}

169		Native  string `json:"native"`  // its native-language translation
170	}

172	// ChunkKind distinguishes streamed tokens from the terminal result/error.
173	type ChunkKind int
174	
175	const (
176		// ChunkToken is one streamed unit of translation text.
177		ChunkToken ChunkKind = iota
178		// ChunkDone carries the final structured result and ends the stream.
179		ChunkDone
180		// ChunkError carries a terminal error and ends the stream.
181		ChunkError
182	)
183	
184	// Chunk is one unit emitted on an engine's result channel.
185	type Chunk struct {
186		Kind   ChunkKind
187		Text   string           // token text (ChunkToken)
188		Result *TranslateResult // final result (ChunkDone)
189		Err    error            // terminal error (ChunkError)
190	}
191	
192	// Engine is the provider abstraction. Every method honors ctx cancellation.
193	//
194	// Translate returns a receive-only channel and MUST close it after sending
195	// exactly one terminal Chunk (ChunkDone or ChunkError). Streaming engines emit
196	// zero or more ChunkToken then one ChunkDone; non-streaming engines emit a
197	// single ChunkDone. This uniform "channel closed == finished" contract lets the
198	// TUI's self-resubscribing reader and the CLI's drain loop share one code path.
199	// The synchronous error return is only for immediate setup failures; all
200	// runtime/network errors flow as a terminal ChunkError.
201	type Engine interface {
202		Name() string
203		Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error)
204		Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error)
205		Available(ctx context.Context) bool
206		Supports(m Mode) bool
207	}
208	
209	// single is a helper for non-streaming engines: it returns a closed channel
210	// carrying exactly one terminal Chunk.
211	func single(res *TranslateResult, err error) <-chan Chunk {
212		ch := make(chan Chunk, 1)
213		if err != nil {
214			ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: err}
215		} else {
216			ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkDone, Result: res}
217		}
218		close(ch)
219		return ch
220	}
221	
222	// Drain consumes an engine channel to completion, returning the final result.
223	// It streams tokens to onToken (if non-nil) as they arrive. Used by the
224	// one-shot CLI path; the TUI drives the channel directly via its Update loop.
225	func Drain(ch <-chan Chunk, onToken func(string)) (*TranslateResult, error) {
226		for c := range ch {
227			switch c.Kind {
228			case ChunkToken:
229				if onToken != nil {
230					onToken(c.Text)
231				}
232			case ChunkDone:
233				return c.Result, nil
234			case ChunkError:
235				return nil, c.Err
236			}
237		}
238		return nil, ErrNoResult
239	}
240

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-22 04:51:28Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
So the issue is in `oneShot`: when streaming was requested but the engine didn't actually stream any tokens (like with dictionary or google engines), it emits a single `ChunkDone` without any `ChunkToken`, which means `onToken` never gets called during the drain phase. Then `oneShot` hits the stream case and just prints a newline, assuming the tokens were already output—but they never were.

The `stream` variable gets set to true based on the conditions (streamPref && TTY && !json && !learn) and passed to the engine, but the smart-auto router sends single words to dictionary, which ignores the Stream flag and returns only a `ChunkDone`. So yes, this is exactly what's happening—the reproduction confirms it: TTY single-word input gives only a newline, while non-TTY gives the translation text, and the only difference is whether `stream` is true.

The fix is to track whether any tokens actually streamed. I'm adding a `streamed` flag that gets set to true whenever the token callback fires, then adjusting the switch logic so that if streaming was requested but nothing came through, we fall back to printing `res.Translation` instead of just the newline. This handles both the LLM case (tokens streamed, print newline) and the dictionary case (no tokens, print the result). I need to verify whether the TTY streaming bug affects other engines like Google too. The core issue is that `case stream:` should check `case stream && streamed:` to handle the zero-token case, and I should confirm that non-streaming engines like Google and Dictionary (which both use `single(...)`) would hit the same problem. Now I'm checking whether the smart-auto and smart-dict engines properly return res.Translation without streaming, and verifying the default stream configuration is set to true. I should also examine google.go and smartauto.go to confirm they handle the single ChunkDone event correctly and don't stream like the LLM path does.

Let me search through the test files to see if there are existing tests for oneShot output that might need updating, then review the engine implementations and any relevant tests before making the fix.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-22 04:53:06Z)**_

All green. Here's the diagnosis and fix.

## 問題根因

單詞翻譯在 CLI（真 TTY）模式下無輸出，是 `cmd/root.go` 的 `oneShot` 串流輸出邏輯 bug。

追蹤流程：
1. 在 TTY 下 `stream = streamPref && stdoutTTY && !flagJSON && !learn` → `true`，程式**請求**逐字串流。
2. 但單詞會被 smart-auto 路由到**字典引擎**（smart-dict）。字典不是 LLM，透過 `single()` 只回傳一個 `ChunkDone`、**零個 `ChunkToken`**（見 `engine.go` 的 Engine 契約）。
3. `Drain` 期間 `onToken` 從未被呼叫 → 譯文完全沒印出來。
4. 最後的 switch 只要 `stream==true` 就命中 `case stream: fmt.Println()`——它**假設 token 已經串流過了**，於是只補一個換行。

結果：TTY 下只輸出一個空的 `\r\n`。管道（非 TTY）因為 `stdoutTTY=false` 使 `stream=false`，走 `default: fmt.Println(res.Translation)`，所以你 `echo directive | translate` 反而正常——這也是為什麼多詞（走 LLM，會真的 emit token）沒事、單詞才壞。

同一個 bug 也會讓 `--engine google`（同樣是 `single()` 非串流）在 TTY 下無輸出。

## 修法

`cmd/root.go` 的 `oneShot`：用 `streamed` 旗標記錄**是否真的有內容被串流出去**，而不是只看有沒有請求串流。請求了串流但零 token 時，退回 `fmt.Println(res.Translation)` 補印完整結果。錯誤路徑的收尾換行也改成只在真的串過內容時才印。

## 驗證（pseudo-TTY 實測）

| 情境 | 修前 | 修後 |
|---|---|---|
| `translate directive`（TTY 單詞） | 只有 `\r\n` | `a. 指导的, 指挥的, 方向的` ✅ |
| `translate directive --to zh-TW`（TTY） | 空 | ✅ |
| `translate "good morning everyone"`（TTY，LLM 串流） | `大家早安` | `大家早安`（無回歸、無重複印）✅ |
| `echo directive \| translate`（管道） | ✅ | ✅ |
| `translate directive --json`（TTY） | ✅ | ✅ |

`go vet ./...` 乾淨，`go test ./...` 全綠。

改動只在 `cmd/root.go` 的 `oneShot`（一個檔案），沒有動引擎層或既有的 LLM 串流行為。要我幫你把這個修正 commit 起來嗎？

The user is asking about shell quoting when translating multi-line text containing double quotes through the CLI—whether the quotes will cause parsing issues, if there's a triple-quote equivalent like Python, or if this is a zsh-specific problem. This is really a shell escaping question rather than a tool-specific issue.

The best solution is using a heredoc with a quoted delimiter, which prevents shell interpolation and lets them paste multi-line text with embedded quotes directly without escaping—essentially the shell's equivalent to Python's triple-quote strings. This avoids the parsing problem entirely by bypassing the command-line argument parsing altogether. e real solution is to sidestep shell quoting altogether by piping stdin — either `pbpaste | translate` on macOS, `translate < file.txt` to read from a file, or using a quoted heredoc `translate <<'EOF'` which acts like a raw string with no interpolation or escaping needed. Since translate reads all of stdin and handles multi-line input well, the bilingual mode might be useful for longer text blocks. _**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-22 05:00:35Z)**_

兩種都成功 — 內嵌 `"`、`'`、多行全部正常翻譯，沒有中斷。

## 結論：這是 zsh 引號問題，不是 `translate` 的問題

`translate` 拿到的是 shell **已經拆好**的參數，或 stdin 的原始 bytes。所以會不會「中斷」，取決於你怎麼餵。

**為什麼 `translate "…"` 會爆掉**：`"` 會提前關閉字串；你的文字裡還有 `it's`、`Mayday's`、`JJ Lin's`（`'` 單引號）跟 `$`、backticks，所以不管用 `"` 還是 `'` 包整段都會斷。這跟 translate 無關，是 shell 在解析引號。

## 解法（依推薦順序）

**1. `pbpaste | translate`（macOS 最推薦）** — 複製那段文字，直接：
```sh
pbpaste | translate --to zh-TW
```
完全繞過 shell 引號，`"`、`'`、`$`、換行通通不用管。你上面那整段直接複製貼上就能翻。

**2. Quoted heredoc — 這就是你說的 Python `"""` 等價物** ✅
```sh
translate --to zh-TW <<'EOF'
The verify loop is now "render, don't just parse."
it's Mayday's, not JJ Lin's, and $HOME `backticks` stay literal.
EOF
```
關鍵是把結束標記**用單引號包起來** `<<'EOF'`。這樣裡面**零跳脫、零變數展開**，`"` `'` `$` backticks 全部照字面送進去 —— 語意上就跟 Python 的 raw triple-quote 一樣。（若寫成不加引號的 `<<EOF`，`$HOME`、backticks 就會被 zsh 展開。）

**3. 檔案** — 最穩，適合超長內容：
```sh
translate --to zh-TW < chord.txt      # 或 pbpaste > chord.txt 先存
```

## 對你這種文件的加分建議

你貼的是**混合文件**（散文 + 檔案路徑 + 程式碼片段 + 中文歌名）。這種用 `--bilingual`（`-2`）doc 模式會更好讀 —— 它保留原文、在每個散文段落下面附上譯文，而路徑/程式碼區塊維持原樣不翻：
```sh
pbpaste | translate -2 --to zh-TW
```

一句話總結：**別用 `translate "整段"`；改用 stdin（`pbpaste |`、`<<'EOF'`、或 `< file`）**，`"` 和多行就都不是問題了。

However, `--` only affects how the program parses arguments; it doesn't change how the shell tokenizes input. The shell still processes quotes, spaces, and newlines before the program ever sees the arguments. So while `--` lets you pass text like `--help` as a literal string, it doesn't solve the problem of unbalanced quotes or multi-line text—those are shell-level issues that `--` can't fix. I'm testing how the CLI handles the `--` separator — when I put `--to zh-TW` after the `--`, it gets treated as literal text rather than a flag, so the entire phrase becomes "good morning everyone --to zh-TW". The translation still went to Chinese, but that seems to be the default behavior rather than the flag being recognized.

The real insight here is that `--` only works at the program level after the shell has already parsed the command line. It can't help with shell-level issues like unbalanced quotes, variable expansion, backticks, pipes, or semicolons — those get interpreted by the shell before the program even sees the arguments. So `--` is purely for letting text that looks like a flag (starts with `-`) pass through as literal text, nothing more.

For arbitrary content with special characters or multiple lines, stdin is still the way to go — whether that's piping from `pbpaste`, using a heredoc, or reading from a file. And the key ordering rule: flags always go before `--`, text always after.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-22 05:03:05Z)**_

實測結果很清楚，來幫你把 `--` 的作用講準：

## `--` 是什麼：end-of-options（選項結束符）

它告訴 `translate` 的**旗標解析器**：「後面別再當旗標了，全部是文字」。

- `translate --zzz` → 被當旗標 → `unknown flag` 報錯
- `translate -- --zzz` → `--zzz` 當文字翻譯（→「呼呼大睡」😄）
- 用途：當你要翻譯的文字本身**以 `-` 開頭**時（例如 `-2`、`--help` 這種字面字串），用 `--` 讓它通過。

⚠️ 注意順序（test 4 可見）：`--` 之後**連 `--to zh-TW` 也會被當成文字**。所以旗標要放在 `--` **前面**：
```sh
translate --to zh-TW -- your text starting with a dash
```

## 但 `--` 救不了你的引號/多行問題

關鍵：**`--` 是 `translate` 在解析，而引號/換行是 zsh 先解析的**。順序是：

```
zsh 拆引號、展開 $、切多行  ──►  translate 才拿到已經切好的 argv（這時 -- 才生效）
```

所以 `--` 對這些**完全無能為力**（因為它們在 translate 看到之前就被 zsh 處理掉了）：

| 情況 | `translate -- …` 能救嗎 |
|---|---|
| 文字以 `-` 開頭 | ✅ 這正是 `--` 的用途 |
| 內嵌 `"` / `'`（不成對） | ❌ zsh 直接卡在 `dquote>` 續行 |
| `$VAR`、backticks `` ` `` | ❌ zsh 先展開 |
| `;` `\|` `&` `*` `(` `)` | ❌ zsh 先解讀成語法 |
| **多行** | ❌ zsh 換行即送出 |

所以你的直覺對 ——「多行就會失敗」，而且不只多行，單行只要有 `"` 或 `$` 也一樣失敗。這跟 `--` 無關。

## 想「後面隨便接」→ 答案還是 stdin

真正能「隨便貼、不管內容」的，是繞過 zsh 引號解析、走 stdin：

```sh
pbpaste | translate --to zh-TW          # 複製後直接翻，最省事
translate --to zh-TW <<'EOF'            # quoted heredoc = Python """ 等價
...隨便貼多行、含 " ' $ ` 都行...
EOF
translate --to zh-TW < file.txt         # 或存檔再翻
```

一句話：**`--` 只解決「文字長得像旗標」，不解決「引號/多行」；後者只能用 stdin。**
